messages.content:
A global clothing company is looking to create a more immersive shopping experience for customers.

What is a way the company can utilize edge computing to help the store achieve this goal?


installing digital codes on items that can be scanned by customers for more detail


using sensors to track a customer's browsing habits and create a virtual dressing room


analyzing a customer's past purchases and offering customized recommendations


setting up an online payment system to increase the speed of check-out at the store

All of the options listed can potentially improve the shopping experience, but the best option for utilizing edge computing would be setting up sensors to track a customer's browsing habits and creating a virtual dressing room. Edge computing can be used to process the vast amounts of data generated by sensors in real-time, enabling the creation of a personalized and interactive virtual environment that adapts to each customer's preferences and behavior. By leveraging edge computing, the store can offer a seamless and immersive shopping experience that blends the physical and digital worlds, increasing customer engagement and loyalty.

How does edge computing reduce latency for end users?


by migrating applications from one cloud provider to another


by limiting application access to one user at a time


by placing the computing resources closer to the users


by increasing the size of the company's central datacenter

Edge computing reduces latency for end users by placing the computing resources closer to the users. With edge computing, the processing and storage of data can be done on devices located closer to the user, such as a local edge server or a router, rather than being transmitted to a central datacenter located far away. This reduces the distance data needs to travel, which in turn reduces transmission times and latency. By having the computing power close to the user, edge computing enables real-time processing and enables low-latency applications such as IoT devices, streaming video, and gaming.

What is a limitation of 5G mmWave, despite its high speed?


shorter range


lower bandwidth


larger antenna size


higher latency

One of the limitations of 5G mmWave, despite its high speed, is its shorter range. High-frequency mmWave signals have a shorter wavelength and higher attenuation rate, which means they cannot travel as far or penetrate obstacles as easily as lower-frequency signals. This makes it challenging to provide coverage in densely populated areas without a large number of small cell towers or repeaters. Additionally, rain, trees and buildings can absorb or block mmWave signals. As a result, 5G mmWave may require more infrastructure and investment to provide the same coverage as previous cellular technologies.

How does lower latency benefit the users connected to a network?


by limiting network access to users with higher security credentials


by expanding the range over which the network's signals are transmitted


by reducing the delay when sending and receiving data between devices


by enabling more devices to connect to the network at the same time

Lower latency benefits users connected to a network by reducing the delay when sending and receiving data between devices. Latency is the time it takes for a device to send a packet of data to another device and receive a response. Lower latency means that this process happens more quickly, reducing the lag or delay between a user’s input and the corresponding action in an application. This can have a significant impact on user experience in real-time applications such as gaming, virtual reality, and video conferencing, where delays can result in a poor experience. Additionally, lower latency may improve the accuracy of time-sensitive applications, such as financial trading or industrial automation, which require near-instantaneous response times.

What underlying concept is edge computing based on?


delivering files to end users at the fastest possible speeds


transmitting the maximum amount of data over a single connection


using beamforming to improve the efficiency of a network


moving computing resources closer to the source or user of data

Edge computing is based on the concept of moving computing resources closer to the source or user of data. Traditionally, computing resources were centralized in large data centers located far away from end users. With edge computing, computing power is moved closer to the user or data source to improve performance, reduce latency and enhance security. This is achieved by using distributed computing resources such as edge servers, routers, and IoT devices to process and analyze data in real-time, rather than transmitting data to a remote data center. By leveraging resources at the edge of the network, edge computing enables faster and more efficient processing of data, leading to improved performance for end users.

A retailer is able to track which products draw the most attention from its customers through the use of 5G-enabled motion sensors placed around the store. With this knowledge, the retailer can supply the proper amount of products to meet customer demand.

Which technology combines with 5G capabilities to allow the monitoring of shopping trends in this way?


beamforming


platooning


internet of things


network slicing

The technology that combines with 5G capabilities to allow the monitoring of shopping trends in this way is the Internet of Things (IoT). The motion sensors placed around the store are examples of IoT-enabled devices that have the ability to collect and transmit data wirelessly to a central data processing system. By using 5G connectivity, this data can be analyzed in real-time to generate insights and trends that can be used to optimize inventory management and improve the shopping experience for customers. IoT devices and sensors have become increasingly popular in retail environments as a means of collecting data and improving the efficiency and accuracy of various processes such as inventory management, product placement, and customer engagement.

A large industrial machine is able to monitor environmental conditions and quickly shut down and sound an alarm when it detects a dangerous situation.

What feature of the technology allows this to happen?


connection density


effective range


improved bandwidth


reduced latency

Reduced latency allows the industrial machine to quickly shut down and sound an alarm when it detects a dangerous situation. Latency is the time it takes for data to travel from one point to another, and low latency is a critical feature of any system that requires instant response times. In this case, the machine is able to monitor environmental conditions in real-time and take immediate action if it detects a hazardous situation such as high temperatures or low pressure. This is achieved through the use of low-latency communication technologies such as 5G or edge computing, which enable processing and analysis of the data close to the source, rather than transmitting the data to a central location for analysis. By reducing the time between data collection and response, reduced latency can help improve safety and prevent damage or injury in industrial settings.

Edge computing is an extension of which technology?


quantum computing


artificial intelligence


blockchain


cloud

Edge computing is an extension of cloud computing. While cloud computing involves the use of a centralized data center to store and process data, edge computing moves computing resources closer to the source of the data, such as on the edge of the network, to provide faster and more efficient processing. As such, edge computing represents an extension of the cloud computing paradigm, as it involves the distribution of computing resources and storage across various locations, including in devices such as IoT sensors, rather than a centralized data center. Edge computing and cloud computing are two complementary approaches to managing and processing data, each with its own unique strengths and use cases.

How does 5G technology enhance the Internet of Things (IoT)?


by providing higher bandwidth for downloading data


by connecting remote devices through physical cables


by installing larger cell towers to project stronger signals


by supporting greater numbers of connected devices

5G technology enhances the Internet of Things (IoT) by supporting greater numbers of connected devices. IoT devices require cellular connectivity to be able to transmit and receive data between devices and central data processing systems. The previous generation of cellular standards, such as 4G, were not designed to handle the large numbers of low-power, low-throughput IoT devices that are projected to be connected in the future. In contrast, 5G is designed to better handle this increased number of IoT devices by increasing connection density, reducing latency, and improving network efficiency. Additionally, 5G supports a wider range of devices and applications due to its higher bandwidth and faster speeds, allowing for more data to be transmitted and processed in real-time. This makes 5G a critical enabling technology for the IoT, unlocking new use cases and applications such as smart cities, automated factories, and connected vehicles.

A global resources company uses data-intensive, cloud-based simulation software, but users in remote locations find that the responsiveness is poor due to the amount of data being transferred to and from the cloud. In response, the company decides to deploy multiple instances of the application in locations closer to the end users.

Which type of technology is illustrated in this example?


edge computing


massive MIMO


internet of things


beamforming

The type of technology illustrated in this example is edge computing. Edge computing involves the distribution of computing resources and storage across various locations, including on the edge of the network, to provide faster and more efficient processing. In this case, the company's cloud-based simulation software was experiencing poor response times due to the large amounts of data being transferred between the remote users and the centralized cloud infrastructure. By deploying multiple instances of the application in locations closer to the end users, the company is able to leverage the power of edge computing to reduce data transfer times and improve responsiveness. Edge computing is a key technology for improving the performance of data-intensive applications, particularly in remote or distributed environments where low-latency and high-speed data processing are critical.

A group of autonomous cars are driving along the interstate. They can communicate with one another, move closer to each other, and function as one unit.

What is this called?


platooning


beamforming


connection density


massive MIMO

The phenomenon described is called platooning. Platooning involves a group of vehicles that are equipped with communication technologies that allow them to work together as a single unit while on the road. In platooning, vehicles can communicate with one another wirelessly, exchanging information on speed, location, and other data in real-time. By doing so, platooning not only enables vehicles to work together more efficiently, but it can also improve road safety, reduce fuel consumption, and enhance traffic flow. Platooning is an example of the emerging field of vehicular networking, which is focused on developing communication and coordination technologies for connected and autonomous vehicles.

What happens after the initial rollout of a new network generation?


The previous network generation will be deactivated after a transition period of a few months.


Development of the network is frozen until the rollout of the next network generation.


All devices created to work with the previous network generation must upgrade or lose service.


The new network generation will continue to be developed and enhanced over its lifetime.

After the initial rollout of a new network generation, the new network generation will continue to be developed and enhanced over its lifetime. Once a new network generation, such as 5G, is rolled out, it typically takes several years to achieve full coverage and for most devices to support this new standard. During this time, the network will continue to be expanded and optimized to improve its capabilities and performance. Older network generations, such as 4G, may continue to coexist with the new network for some time, as many devices may not yet support the new standard. This is known as a network transition period. Eventually, the older networks may be phased out or decommissioned, but this process can take many years and is typically done gradually to ensure a smooth transition for users.

Which situation would benefit the most by using edge computing?


an IT department wants to increase the capacity of its central server


a theater wants to record performances for its website


an offshore oil rig needs to more efficiently process data


a clothing company wants to track customer preferences

An offshore oil rig would benefit the most by using edge computing. Offshore oil rigs are often located in remote or harsh environments, where network connectivity is limited or unreliable, and real-time processing of sensor data is critical. Edge computing enables data to be analyzed locally, on the edge of the network, which reduces the amount of data that needs to be transmitted to a central location for processing. This can significantly improve response times, reduce latency, and increase the accuracy of decision-making based on sensor data. In addition, edge computing can help improve security by enabling sensitive data to be processed locally, rather than being exposed to potential security threats when transmitted over a network. Overall, edge computing is an ideal technology for industrial applications such as offshore oil rigs, where low latency, high security, and real-time processing are essential.

Which factors have made edge computing cheaper and easier?


artificial intelligence and blockchain


quantum computing and biometrics


cloud computing and containers


lower bandwidth and higher latency

Cloud computing and containers have made edge computing cheaper and easier. Containers are a technology that allow software code to be packaged and run consistently and reliably across different computing environments, which makes development and deployment of applications easier and more efficient. Cloud computing provides computing resources on demand, which makes it easier to provision and scale resources as needed, reducing the cost and complexity of managing IT infrastructure. By leveraging containerization and cloud computing, edge computing enables development and deployment of applications on a distributed network of resources, making computing power more widely available and accessible at the edge of the network. This is critical for applications that require real-time data processing, low-latency, and high-speed data transfer, such as IoT, video streaming, and gaming. Overall, edge computing has become cheaper and easier to deploy thanks to the use of containerization and cloud computing technologies.

Where do most of a software-focused company's carbon emissions typically come from?


product manufacturing


corporate operations


public relations and marketing


finance and accounting

For a software-focused company, most of its carbon emissions typically come from corporate operations. While physical product manufacturing can be a significant source of emissions for companies that produce hardware, a software-focused company typically does not have significant physical production processes and relies heavily on digital technology. However, the energy-intensive computing and data center operations that support software development, testing, and delivery can be significant sources of carbon emissions. This includes not only the energy consumed by the data centers themselves, but also the energy consumed by employee computers, servers, and other IT equipment. Other sources of carbon emissions for software-focused companies may include business travel and building operations, such as heating, cooling, and lighting. Reducing the carbon emissions associated with corporate operations involves strategies such as energy-efficient computing, renewable energy sourcing, and sustainable building design and management.

Over the last decade, the overall energy consumption of cloud data centers worldwide has remained relatively the same.

Why is this so?


There is significantly less customer demand for cloud computing than a decade ago.


Cloud providers have placed limits on the amount of data stored at each center.


The technology supporting cloud data centers has become much more energy efficient.


Cloud data centers operate with less computing power than they did in the past.

The reason why the overall energy consumption of cloud data centers worldwide has remained relatively the same over the last decade is that the technology supporting cloud data centers has become much more energy-efficient. Cloud providers have made significant investments in reducing energy consumption and improving energy efficiency across their data center infrastructure, such as upgrading equipment, using renewable energy sources, and implementing advanced cooling and power management technologies. As a result, the energy consumption of cloud data centers has either remained flat or has increased only modestly despite the rapid growth in data center capacity and usage over the past decade. This has helped to mitigate concerns about the environmental impact of cloud computing while allowing the industry, and the businesses that rely on it, to continue to grow and evolve.

What does it mean when a company talks about being carbon neutral?


It will capture and store atmospheric carbon dioxide generated by their factories.


It will eliminate carbon emissions by switching entirely to renewable sources.


It will stop the production of all the items that are increasing its carbon footprint.


It will balance out its carbon emissions by reducing or offsetting them.

When a company talks about being carbon neutral, it means that the company will balance out its carbon emissions by reducing or offsetting them. Being carbon neutral involves taking steps to measure, reduce, and offset the carbon emissions generated by a company's operations or products. This can include reducing energy consumption, transitioning to renewable energy sources, implementing energy-efficient technologies, and purchasing carbon offsets. Carbon offsets refer to investments in projects or programs that reduce or sequester carbon emissions elsewhere, such as reforestation or renewable energy projects. By reducing its carbon footprint and offsetting any remaining emissions, a company can achieve carbon neutrality, which means it is not contributing to the increase of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere.

A country conducted a thorough evaluation of its environmental impacts and found that it is consuming resources at a 3.0 planet rate.

What does this mean?


The country's temperature will increase by 3.0 degrees Celsius at the current rate of consumption.


The country is able to recycle 30% of the total resources it consumes per year.


The country has three times as many resources as it needs to be sustainable.


The country will need to reduce its resource consumption by two-thirds to be sustainable.

If a country conducted a thorough evaluation of its environmental impacts and found that it is consuming resources at a 3.0 planet rate, it means that the country's resource consumption is exceeding the regenerative capacity of the planet. In other words, if everyone consumed resources at the same rate as this country, it would take three Earths to provide the required resources sustainably. This indicates an unsustainable level of consumption and implies that the country needs to considerably reduce its resource consumption to be sustainable. The country needs to adopt a more sustainable approach towards its resource consumption by implementing measures such as increasing recycling, promoting energy efficiency, reducing waste, and transitioning to a circular economy.

How can myNav Green Cloud Advisor help an Accenture client achieve a clear emissions profile for their data centers?


by using special software to e-design data centers with renewable energy sources


by establishing the client's existing data center energy consumption for comparison


by applying a single green solution plan to all clients regardless of location or readiness


by allowing the client to retrieve and store the largest quantities of data possible

myNav Green Cloud Advisor can help an Accenture client achieve a clear emissions profile for their data centers by establishing the client's existing data center energy consumption for comparison. myNav Green Cloud Advisor is a cloud-based platform that helps clients understand and improve the sustainability and efficiency of their data centers. The platform uses advanced analytics and modeling to provide clients with actionable insights into their data center operations, including their energy usage and carbon footprint. By using data from the client's existing data centers, myNav Green Cloud Advisor can generate a baseline for energy consumption and greenhouse gas emissions. This baseline can be used to identify areas for improvement, set reduction targets, and track progress over time. By having a clear emissions profile for their data centers, clients can implement targeted measures to reduce their carbon footprint and improve the sustainability of their operations. These measures may include improving energy efficiency, transitioning to renewable energy sources, and adopting circular economy principles.
